semantic.question: Which description best matches the overall 3D shape of the object?
options: A perfect cylinder with a domed top
A rectangular block with a cylindrical boss on top
A solid stadium-shaped profile (elongated with semi-circular ends) extruded to a uniform thickness
An elliptical ring with a hollow center
semantic.evidence: The render and sketch show a single continuous body formed by extruding a profile made of two parallel lines connected by two semi-circles.

semantic.question: Which statement correctly describes the internal features of the object?
options: It features a hollow interior with thin outer walls
It is a completely solid body with no holes, pockets, or internal cavities
It contains a single through-hole in the center
It has a rectangular pocket cut into its top face
semantic.evidence: The object is a simple extrusion of a closed outer loop with no internal boundaries or subtractive features.

semantic.question: Looking at the primary cross-section (the largest flat face), what is its symmetry?
options: It has two orthogonal axes of mirror symmetry
It has radial symmetry (rotational symmetry of infinite order)
It is completely asymmetrical
It has exactly one axis of mirror symmetry
semantic.evidence: The stadium shape has mirror symmetry along both its longitudinal (lengthwise) and transverse (widthwise) axes.

semantic.question: Counting all surfaces on the object, how many flat, planar faces are present?
options: Zero (the entire object is continuously curved)
Exactly six (forming a complete rectangular box)
Exactly two (only the top and bottom)
Exactly four (the top, bottom, and two parallel side walls)
semantic.evidence: The object has a flat top and bottom face, plus two flat side faces corresponding to the straight line segments of the profile. The ends are curved cylindrical faces.

param.question: What is the distance between the centers of the two semicircular ends of the object?
What is the ratio of the object's overall length to its overall width?
What is the radius of the circular through-hole in the center of the object?
options: There is no circular through-hole; the object is completely solid
param.evidence: The object is a solid stadium shape with no internal holes. This is a Type B existence trap to catch models that hallucinate a central hole.

param.question: How many distinct curved (cylindrical) side faces does the final object have?
param.evidence: The object has two semicircular ends, resulting in exactly 2 curved side faces.

param.question: What best describes the 2D profile shape that was extruded to create this object?
options: An ellipse
A pure circle
A stadium shape consisting of two parallel straight lines connected by two semicircular arcs
A rectangle with sharp corners
param.evidence: The sketch consists of two straight lines and two arcs, forming a stadium (pill) shape, which is then extruded.

param.question: What is the overall extruded height (thickness) of the object?
param.evidence: The extrusion depth (height) of the object is 0.15.

param.question: What is the radius of the semicircular curved ends of the object?
options: The object does not have curved ends; it is a simple rectangle
param.evidence: arc_midpoint_x=0.75, arc_start_x=0.5625, radius=0.75-0.5625=0.1875. Option D is a Type A existence trap.

param.question: What is the sum of the overall length and the overall width of the object?
